mainEntity.name: What can I do if spoken search keeps misunderstanding certain words, names, or my accent?

acceptedAnswer.text:
Spoken search can struggle with names, slang, and accents, but you can improve results by spelling tricky words aloud or adding context.
For example, say “Show reviews for restaurant spelled N-O-B-U in downtown Denver.”

**Key factors:**
- Rarity of the word or brand name
- Accent and pronunciation differences
- System training data for local terminology
- Willingness to spell or rephrase

When assistants routinely mishear a particular phrase, create a shortcut or voice command if your device supports it.
In cities like Los Angeles or San Diego with diverse accents and languages, speak a bit slower and avoid overlapping speech to give the system a clean signal.
acceptedAnswer.description: When spoken search mishears you, slow down, reduce noise, and add context or spell difficult names aloud. You can also create shortcuts for common commands and, in multilingual or accent-heavy environments, keep queries short and clearly enunciated.
